column2: Complete the 🚪 Attack Vectors sheet first to pre-populate this column's dropdown!
column3: Describe in detail a scenario chaining these TTPs together to result in TA accessing 👑 Key Asset.

column2: T1190: Exploit Public-Facing Application, T1078: Valid Accounts, T1005: Data from Local System
column3: An attacker exploits a vulnerability on a public-facing web server.The attacker uses stolen credentials to access the vendor's development environment.They locate and exfiltrate sensitive data.

column2: T1078: Valid Accounts, T1005: Data from Local System
column3: A company employee uses credentials to download company IP and sell it to competitors.

column2: T1649: Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates, T1505: Server Software Component, T1005: Data from Local System
column3: TA uses source code gathered from .git folder to inject malware. Malware steals credit card information.

column2: T1040: Network Sniffing, T1078: Valid Accounts, T1005: Data from Local System
column3: The attacker gains access to a network segment and begins passively capturing network traffic. The attacker identifies a user's credentials from the sniff network traffic. Using the compromised credentials, and accesses sensitive IP data.
